name: Project architecture overview
description: pi-agent-orchestrator is a Pi package/extension for explicit multi-agent orchestration. extensions/multi-agent/index.ts registers Pi tools, slash commands, and session lifecycle hooks; extensions/multi-agent/server.ts serves the React dashboard plus REST/SSE APIs; extensions/multi-agent/spawn.ts creates bwrap-isolated child Pi RPC agents in git worktrees; extensions/delegate-agent.ts is copied into sub-agent worktrees to provide sibling delegation. The dashboard is static React/TypeScript in web/ and is built by `bun run build`.

title: Orchestration is explicit and dashboard-backed, not TUI-invasive
rationale: The root Pi session only creates sub-agents after `/orchestrate` enables orchestration mode. The extension uses a headless HTTP server with REST and SSE for the browser dashboard and intentionally avoids modifying the Pi TUI, keeping the terminal responsive and clean.

title: Orchestrator Libraries are user-owned namespaced resource repos
rationale: Version-controlled agent types/templates should not be stored in the package directory. A configured Orchestrator Library is a user/team folder with a valid orchestrator-library.json manifest. The manifest name is a unique namespace; duplicate library names are rejected. Orchestrator resource refs use readable colon syntax such as `team-ai:skills/code-review`, while package-shipped resources remain read-only examples/fallbacks.

name: Orchestrator Library Phase 1 implementation map
description: Orchestrator Library backend foundation lives in extensions/multi-agent/orchestrator-library.ts. It parses/validates orchestrator-library.json, enforces unique manifest namespaces across configured libraries, reads/writes piAgentOrchestrator.libraries settings, discovers resources from manifest-defined directories, bootstraps starter libraries, and exposes discovery through /api/orchestrator-libraries plus dashboard read/onboarding UI in web/app.tsx.

name: Extension template smoke tests use temporary agents plus runtime tool snapshots
description: Dashboard validation for extension templates should spawn a throwaway Pi RPC agent with the resolved template extensions, wait briefly for the delegate extension's runtime-tools.json snapshot, report tool conflicts/diagnostics to the dashboard, then kill the process and remove its worktree without adding it to the live agents map.

title: Package examples are hidden via project display settings, not removal
rationale: The package pio-example resources remain shipped and visible by default for onboarding. Users can hide them per project with piAgentOrchestrator.showPackageExamples=false; dashboard APIs filter read-only package example agent types from listing only, while underlying discovery remains available for code paths that need it.

title: New agent/template create flows target Orchestrator Libraries when configured
rationale: Agent definitions and skill/extension templates are now discovered from configured Orchestrator Libraries and save to the first valid library's manifest-configured resource directory when a library exists. Legacy project .pi folders remain readable/fallback for existing resources and no-library setups, but configured libraries are the preferred write target.

title: Native Pi resource paths are hidden behind Orchestrator Libraries
rationale: The dashboard no longer exposes Skill & Extension Paths as a primary tab. Raw Pi skills/extensions settings remain available only as an advanced native escape hatch inside the Orchestrator Libraries panel, while extension discovery now includes Orchestrator Library extensions after native/npm sources so library-managed extension templates are the preferred path.
